reasoning: The Oct 9, 2025 ceasefire based on the 20-point Trump peace plan has a Phase Two that explicitly requires Hamas to disarm. US Envoy Steve Witkoff claims Hamas privately agreed to this, and an internationally supervised weapons 'buyback/decommissioning' program is set to launch by late November or December. If this program launches and Hamas members participate under leadership's tacit or explicit approval, the resolution's fallback clause ('wide consensus of credible reporting confirming a policy of disarmament has been instituted') could trigger by Dec 31. However, Hamas has publicly rejected disarmament as recently as Aug/Sept 2025, and their military wing remains opposed. Given how rarely armed factions quickly relinquish arms and the tight 7-week window to successfully launch Phase Two and secure wide verification, the odds of a formal or universally recognized disarmament policy being instituted before 2026 remain low. I estimate 17%, slightly lower than the Oct 30 market price of 21% due to the compression of the timeline and persistent on-the-ground resistance from Hamas's military wing.
